Politics.headline: DC gala shooting suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump
Politics.detail: Cole Tomas Allen, who allegedly tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner with weapons, has been charged with attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump and faces a potential life sentence. Authorities suggest the attack was planned for several weeks.

Politics.headline: Republicans in Congress push for Trump's White House ballroom after shooting at media dinner
Politics.detail: Republicans in Congress are seeking approval and funding for President Trump's proposed White House ballroom, arguing it would enhance security following the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner. A new bill proposes allocating $400 million for construction and security infrastructure.

Politics.headline: Romanian opposition parties move to bring down government
Politics.detail: Romania's Social Democratic Party (PSD) and the Alliance for the Union of Romanians (AUR) are submitting a joint motion of censure against the government led by Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan. This action marks an escalation in the country's political crisis after the Social Democrats withdrew from the ruling coalition.

Politics.headline: Trump being 'humiliated' in Iran talks, Germany's Merz says
Politics.detail: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz stated that the U.S. is being “humiliated” by Iranian leaders as President Trump struggles to negotiate an end to the war, potentially straining transatlantic relations. Merz added he doesn't see what strategic exit the Americans are choosing and that Tehran's negotiators are proceeding very skillfully.

Politics.headline: Altaf Bukhari urges Govt to reconsider ban on Siraj-ul-Uloom
Politics.detail: Altaf Bukhari has called on the government to reconsider its ban on Siraj-ul-Uloom. The specific reasons for the ban and Bukhari's arguments were not detailed in the provided context.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK faces higher prices for eight months after war in Iran ends, says minister
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK is expected to face higher prices for eight months following the end of the war in Iran, according to a minister.

Finance & Economy.headline: Markets rise as Iran crisis stays in limbo
Finance & Economy.detail: Market conditions are improving as the Iran crisis remains uncertain.

Finance & Economy.headline: RBI finalises new risk weight norms
Finance & Economy.detail: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has finalized new risk weight norms.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil drops sharply on opening of Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices have decreased significantly following the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Finance & Economy.headline: Putin's oil windfall won't revive his slowing wartime economy.
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite increased revenue from oil, Russia's wartime economy continues to slow.

Technology.headline: Apple and Google crushed California bill helping smaller rivals
Technology.detail: Some of the world's largest technology companies swiftly quashed a California legislative measure, called the “Based Act,” that pitted giants such as Apple and Google against smaller rivals, a display of their immense political influence in their home state; the bill sought to stop major technology platforms from favoring their own products over those of competitors in places such as Apple's App Store.

Technology.headline: Accenture to roll out Copilot to all 743,000 employees in boost for Microsoft
Technology.detail: Microsoft is rolling out its Copilot 365 AI assistant to all of Accenture's roughly 743,000 employees, in the biggest enterprise deal for the chatbot as the software giant seeks to convert more of its vast customer base into paying users. Accenture said the initial Copilot deployment has paid off, with 97% of staff saying it helped them complete routine tasks up to 15 times faster.

Technology.headline: Roundtable Founders, Tech Platform Visionaries unite, to launch the Impossible. AI Powered, DeFi Media Platform at the "Possible” 2026 Media Conference
Technology.detail: RYVYL Inc. and RTB Digital announced that tech platform powerhouses have united to launch the media industry's only full-stack AI + DeFi + Web3 platform at the "Possible” Conference in Miami this week. RTB's platform can replace and fund the entire operational/tech stack, including data layer, IP-security, CMS, ad/operations, reporting and payments, internal and external syndication, monetization, editorial management and business intelligence, hosting, end-to-end video stack, and "App-stack” integration.

Technology.headline: This hidden TV feature tracks your viewing - here's how to turn it off (no matter what brand) in 1 minute
Technology.detail: Many smart TVs have a setting that tracks your viewing habits. Here's how to disable that feature in about a minute.

Technology.headline: Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman head to court in high-stakes showdown over AI
Technology.detail: Technology tycoons Elon Musk and Sam Altman are poised to face off in a high-stakes trial revolving around the alleged betrayal, deceit and unbridled ambition that blurred the bickering billionaires' once-shared vision for the development of artificial intelligence.

Science.headline: NASA rover uncovers rock with 7 new organic molecules on Mars — the 'most diverse collection' ever seen
Science.detail: The Mars rover has discovered a rock containing the most diverse collection of organic molecules ever found on the red planet, suggesting potentially habitable conditions may have existed in the past.

Science.headline: Risk of early death 60% higher in people with bipolar II disorder, population-based study reveals
Science.detail: A recent population-based study has revealed that individuals with bipolar II disorder face a 60% higher risk of early death.

Science.headline: Infrasound Can Subtly Raise Stress and Discomfort, New Study Finds
Science.detail: New research indicates that infrasound, which is very low-frequency sound below 20 Hz, can elevate cortisol levels and cause irritability, potentially explaining why some 'haunted' locations feel unsettling.

Science.headline: Honeybees Can Process Numerical Information, New Study Confirms
Science.detail: A new study confirms that honeybees possess the ability to process numerical information.

Science.headline: Sewers have been hiding a climate problem in plain sight, and this new tool finally exposes its true scale
Science.detail: A new tool has exposed the true scale of a climate problem hidden in sewers.

Health.headline: UNICEF warns Afghanistan could lose up to 25,000 female health workers, teachers
Health.detail: Afghanistan is at risk of losing more than 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030 due to Taliban restrictions on girls' education and women's employment, according to a UNICEF report. These restrictions have already affected at least 1 million girls and could double by 2030 if the situation doesn't change.

Health.headline: Practicing today for tomorrow's emergencies – WHO convenes countries and partners to simulate response to major disease outbreak
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) is holding a meeting with countries and partners to simulate responses to major disease outbreaks, ensuring preparedness for future health emergencies. This initiative aims to improve global readiness and coordination in the face of potential pandemics.

Health.headline: Automated Insulin Delivery Cleared for Pregnant T1D Patients
Health.detail: Medscape Medical News reports on the clearance of an automated insulin delivery system for pregnant women with type 1 diabetes. This development could significantly improve glycemic control and maternal and fetal outcomes in this high-risk population.

Health.headline: This Hidden Kind of Stress May Be Damaging Your Memory as You Age
Health.detail: A new study indicates that internalizing stress, particularly feelings of hopelessness, can accelerate memory decline in older Chinese Americans. Community support did not show the same impact, according to researchers.

Health.headline: UK spring sunshine prompts warnings over unsafe fake designer sunglasses
Health.detail: The Guardian reports on warnings issued regarding the use of unsafe, fake designer sunglasses as spring sunshine arrives in the UK. These sunglasses may not provide adequate UV protection, posing a risk to eye health.

Sports.headline: Gin Kings face tough stretch after whipping lowly Risers
Sports.detail: The Gin Kings are preparing for a challenging series of games after their victory over the Risers.

Sports.headline: NBA: Jokic fined $50,000, Randle $35K for Nuggets-Wolves spat
Sports.detail: Nikola Jokic and Julius Randle received fines for their involvement in an altercation during the Nuggets-Timberwolves game.

Sports.headline: NBA: Lakers' Deandre Ayton ejected after 'excessive' elbow to Sengun
Sports.detail: Deandre Ayton was ejected from the Lakers game after delivering an 'excessive' elbow to Alperen Sengun.

Sports.headline: Spurs star Wembanyama returns, says he's disappointed with the way his concussion was handled
Sports.detail: Victor Wembanyama has returned to the court after recovering from a concussion, but expressed disappointment with how the situation was managed.

Sports.headline: NFL draft first round averages 13.2 million viewers on television and digital platforms
Sports.detail: The first round of the NFL draft drew an average of 13.2 million viewers across television and digital platforms.

Entertainment.headline: Michael Jackson streams skyrocket after 'Michael' biopic opening weekend, up 95% in the US
Entertainment.detail: Fans of the King of Pop are eagerly consuming his music, with streams increasing significantly after the biopic's release.

Entertainment.headline: Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson Split
Entertainment.detail: Reports indicate that Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson have ended their relationship.

Entertainment.headline: Are Zoe Kravitz & Harry Styles Engaged? What We Know So Far
Entertainment.detail: Zoë Kravitz was spotted with a ring, sparking engagement rumors with Harry Styles.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z addresses allegations related to Sean 'Diddy' Combes' 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit in a new 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Valerie Perrine, 'Superman' and 'Lenny' Actress, Dead at 82
Entertainment.detail: Valerie Perrine, known for her roles in 'Superman' and 'Lenny', has passed away at the age of 82 after battling Parkinson's for several years.

Crime & Justice.headline: US press gala shooting suspect charged with attempting to kill Trump
Crime & Justice.detail: The suspect in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner has been charged with attempting to kill President Trump and other US officials, according to authorities.

Crime & Justice.headline: Islamic State militants kill at least 29 in an attack on a village in northeastern Nigeria
Crime & Justice.detail: At least 29 people were killed in an attack by Islamic State militants on a village in northeastern Nigeria.

Crime & Justice.headline: 2 people found dead in Langley, homicide team called in
Crime & Justice.detail: After two people were discovered dead in Langley, a homicide team has been called in to investigate the situation.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect in White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Charged with Attempt to Assassinate the President
Crime & Justice.detail: The individual suspected of perpetrating the shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner has been formally charged with attempting to assassinate the President.

Crime & Justice.headline: 3rd man charged in Jam Master Jay murder changes plea to guilty.
Crime & Justice.detail: A third man who was charged in the Jam Master Jay murder case has changed his plea to guilty.

Education.headline: SEND reform: putting children and young people first
Education.detail: The UK Department for Education is seeking views on updates to guidance to strengthen how schools support pupils with medical conditions and allergies. This open consultation aims to prioritize the needs of children and young people in special education needs and disabilities (SEND) reforms.

Education.headline: Every child achieving and thriving
Education.detail: The Department for Education published a policy paper outlining strategies to ensure every child can achieve and thrive. This initiative likely includes various support systems and educational reforms.

Education.headline: Assam HS Result 2026 to be announced on April 28, confirms Himanta Biswa Sarma
Education.detail: The Assam Class 12 HS Result 2026 is set to be announced on April 28, and students will be able to access their results on official websites. The confirmed date has ended the wait for lakhs of candidates.

Education.headline: Labour MP 'unpersuaded' by calls to give universities more money
Education.detail: A Labour MP has expressed skepticism regarding calls to increase funding for universities. This suggests ongoing debate and scrutiny over higher education financing in the UK.

Education.headline: Plans to axe 600 jobs mooted at Nottingham to save £50 million
Education.detail: The University of Nottingham is considering cutting 600 jobs in an effort to save £50 million. This indicates financial challenges within the higher education sector.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Subtle but powerful form of self-validation': how to start journaling
Lifestyle.detail: An article discusses the benefits of journaling and how to start the practice as a form of self-validation.

Lifestyle.headline: A job that changed me: I spent a year reading electrical meters – and rediscovered the joy of writing
Lifestyle.detail: A writer reflects on how a year spent reading electrical meters led to rediscovering the joy of writing.

Lifestyle.headline: Report: GM Is Slowing Down its EV Plans
Lifestyle.detail: General Motors is reportedly slowing down its electric vehicle plans.

Lifestyle.headline: Why Consumer Reports called the acura slx 'Not acceptable'
Lifestyle.detail: An article discusses why Consumer Reports gave the Acura SLX a 'Not Acceptable' rating.

Lifestyle.headline: India's butterfly boom: How Nature walks, parks, and trails are sparking a new conservation movement
Lifestyle.detail: Nature walks, parks and trails are contributing to a butterfly boom and sparking a new conservation movement in India.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: It's time to address psychosocial hazards in a changing world of work
Opinion/Editorial.detail: To mark World Day for Safety and Health at Work, Kaori Nakamura-Osaka, International Labour Organization Assistant Director-General and Regional Director for Asia and the Pacific, calls for stronger action to protect workers from psychosocial hazards, as these risks are not inevitable and arise from identifiable aspects of work that can be changed with commitment from governments, employers and workers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: SBP Raises Policy Rate by 100 bps in Measured Tightening
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The State Bank of Pakistan increased its policy rate by 100 bps to prevent inflation expectations from worsening due to high oil prices and domestic inflation. This decision reflects a timely recalibration in a volatile global environment, balancing the need to anchor expectations with the aim of not choking a still-recovering economy.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: SMEs and the talent retention riddle
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Ministry of Labor data showed that fewer fresh graduates are entering the workforce, with a declining proportion choosing to work for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), which account for the majority of Taiwanese businesses and employment. SMEs face challenges including financial support, digital transformation, and talent shortages while larger corporations attract talent with better salaries and career opportunities.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Cherry blossoms are proof of a planet going awry
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Cherry blossoms in Washington D.C. are blooming earlier, with the peak bloom date shifting about eight days earlier since the 1940s, which serves as a bellwether for the effects of climate change on plant life and agriculture. This early blooming, alongside droughts, wildfires and destructive storms, signal a planet going awry.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Lessons on impunity
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The International Criminal Court (ICC) will proceed with a trial for former Philippine President Rodrigo Roa Duterte for alleged crimes against humanity, as the ICC rejected challenges to its jurisdiction and confirmed the charges against Duterte. This landmark decision allows the pursuit of accountability before an impartial court, giving Duterte the right to present his defense.

World.headline: Trains collide outside Jakarta, killing at least seven
World.detail: At least seven people were killed and dozens injured after two trains collided outside of Jakarta, Indonesia. Rescuers are working to reach any remaining survivors trapped in the wreckage.

World.headline: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
World.detail: A bomb blast on a highway in Colombia has resulted in the death of 21 people amidst ongoing pre-election violence.

World.headline: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes kill 14 in deadliest day since ceasefire began
World.detail: Israeli strikes in Lebanon have killed 14 people, marking the deadliest day since the ceasefire began, according to the Lebanon health ministry.

World.headline: US press gala shooting suspect charged with attempting to kill Trump
World.detail: A suspect has been charged with attempting to assassinate Donald Trump at the White House correspondents' dinner shooting.

World.headline: NATO alliance considers end to annual summits
World.detail: NATO is considering ending its practice of holding annual summits, potentially to avoid a tense encounter with the U.S. President. Some members are pushing to hold summits every two years.

Zimbabwe.headline: Afreximbank $3bn facility to provide cheaper fuel via Namibia
Zimbabwe.detail: Afreximbank has provided a $3 billion facility to facilitate cheaper fuel imports via Namibia, potentially easing Zimbabwe's fuel costs.

Zimbabwe.headline: Inclusive development way to go — VP Chiwenga
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President Chiwenga has emphasized that inclusive development is the path forward for Zimbabwe, ensuring that all citizens benefit from the country's progress.

Zimbabwe.headline: Decentralising Uhuru Day a game-changer: VP Mohadi
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President Mohadi stated that decentralizing Uhuru Day (Independence Day) is a significant move that will transform the celebrations and bring them closer to the people.

Zimbabwe.headline: ARE STUDENTS BEING CONSUMED BY DRUGS AND SUBSTANCE ABUSE AT OUR UNIVERSITIES?
Zimbabwe.detail: An investigation into the prevalence of drug and substance abuse among students at Zimbabwean universities is underway, raising concerns about the well-being of the younger generation.

Zimbabwe.headline: INFANT DROWNS AFTER MUM SLIPS WHILE CROSSING FLOODED RIVER
Zimbabwe.detail: A two-year-old child drowned after being swept away by strong currents when her mother slipped while crossing a flooded river, highlighting the dangers posed by extreme weather events.
